heading: Omitted
label: 1401 to 1407 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection 1401, , , authorized an appropriation of $1,800,000 to accomplish the purposes declared in former provisions of this subchapter and provided for payments by Secretary of Treasury upon order of Secretary of Agriculture.act Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 30, title IV, § 40152 Stat. 70Section 1402, , , provided for deposit of appropriation to credit of the Secretary of Agriculture for disbursement for purposes stated in former provisions of this subchapter.act Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 30, title IV, § 40252 Stat. 70Section 1403, acts , title IV, § 403, ; , , provided allotment of funds to manager of cotton pool for purchase of pool participation trust certificates, to be tendered by lawful holder and owner thereof on or before , at rate of $1 per five-hundred-pound bale, payment of costs and expenses incident to such purchases and covering into the Treasury as miscellaneous receipts balance remaining at expiration of purchase period.Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 3052 Stat. 70Apr. 7, 1938, ch. 107, § 1352 Stat. 204May 1, 1938Section 1404, acts , title IV, § 404, ; , , extended the time limit for purchase of outstanding pool participation certificates to and including , authorized issuance of rules and regulations and prohibited purchases from other than record holders on or before .Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 3052 Stat. 71Apr. 7, 1938, ch. 107, § 1452 Stat. 204July 31, 1938May 1, 1938Section 1404a, acts , title I, ; , , extended the time limit for purchase of certificates to and including and made the date of inapplicable.June 16, 1938, ch. 46452 Stat. 747Apr. 5, 1939, ch. 4453 Stat. 572Sept. 30, 1939May 1, 1938Section 1404b, , , provided for issuance of regulations for payments on participation trust certificates in case of death, incompetence or disappearance of payee.act June 16, 1938, ch. 464, title I52 Stat. 747Section 1405, , , authorized continuance of 1933 cotton producers pool as long as necessary to effectuate purposes of former provisions of this subchapter and use of funds for payment of expenses.act Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 30, title IV, § 40552 Stat. 71Section 1406, , , prohibited purchase of certificates after expiration of time limit.act Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 30, title IV, § 40652 Stat. 71Section 1407, acts , title IV, § 407, ; , , provided for payment by assignee of certificate transferred subsequent to , limited to the purchase price paid by the assignee, with interest at rate of four per centum from date of purchase, not exceeding an amount of $1 per bale, payment to be based upon affidavit of assignee.Feb. 16, 1938, ch. 3052 Stat. 71Apr. 7, 1938, ch. 107, § 1552 Stat. 204May 1, 1937

Inapplicability of SubchapterSubchapter, with the exception of former sections 1404a and 1404b, inapplicable to 2002 through 2007 crops of covered commodities, peanuts, and sugar and inapplicable to milk during period beginning , through , see .May 13, 2002Dec. 31, 2007section 7992(a)(4) of this titleSubchapter, with the exception of former sections 1404a and 1404b, inapplicable to 1996 through 2001 crops of loan commodities, peanuts, and sugar and inapplicable to milk during period beginning , and ending , see .Apr. 4, 1996Dec. 31, 2002section 7301(a)(1)(I) of this title

Settlement of Certain Claims and Accounts, §§ 2, 3, , authorized Comptroller General to relieve disbursing and certifying officers from liability for payments made under former provisions of this subchapter upon certificate of Secretary of Agriculture that such payments were made in good faith, and also provided that no action should be taken to recover such excess payments, if the Secretary of Agriculture should further certify that, in view of the good faith of the parties or other circumstances of the case, such attempt to recover them would be inadvisable or inequitable.Act June 5, 1942, ch. 34956 Stat. 324

Settlement of Certain Claims and Accounts, §§ 2, 3, , authorized Comptroller General to relieve disbursing and certifying officers from liability for payments made under former provisions of this subchapter upon certificate of Secretary of Agriculture that such payments were made in good faith, and also provided that no action should be taken to recover such excess payments, if the Secretary of Agriculture should further certify that, in view of the good faith of the parties or other circumstances of the case, such attempt to recover them would be inadvisable or inequitable.Act June 5, 1942, ch. 34956 Stat. 324
